Acute myocardial infarction (STEMI)
The BAG3 gene variant NM_004281.4(BAG3):c.626C>T (p.Pro209Leu) may contribute to the pathogenesis of acute myocardial infarction through disrupted sarcomere function.

The MYBPC3 gene variant NM_000256.3(MYBPC3):c.3392T>C (p.Ile1131Thr) could be associated with an increased risk of cardiac arrest and cardiovascular phenotype in patients with acute myocardial infarction.

The presence of multiple BAG3 gene variants, including NM_004281.4(BAG3):c.626C>T (p.Pro209Leu), may indicate a genetic predisposition to dilated cardiomyopathy 1HH and myofibrillar myopathy 6 in patients with acute myocardial infarction.
Coronary artery disease with acute plaque rupture
ENPP1 variant NM_006208.3(ENPP1):c.1025G>T disrupts ectonucleotide pyrophosphatase activity, promoting arterial calcification and plaque instability.
PKP2 variants NM_001005242.3(PKP2):c.1418A>G or c.464G>C may compromise cardiac connexin-43 function, predisposing to coronary artery disease with acute plaque rupture.
PRKAG2 variant NM_016203.4(PRKAG2):c.471C>T or c.1098A>G could lead to aberrant AMP-activated protein kinase activity, exacerbating atherosclerotic plaque formation and instability.

The presence of multiple BAG3 gene variants, including NM_004281.4(BAG3):c.626C>T (p.Pro209Leu), may indicate a genetic predisposition to dilated cardiomyopathy 1HH and myofibrillar myopathy 6 in patients with acute myocardial infarction.
The presence of NM_004281.4(BAG3):c.626C>T (p.Pro209Leu) may indicate a higher risk of myofibrillar myopathy 6 in patients with acute myocardial infarction.
The conflicting pathogenicity classifications for BAG3 variants, including NM_004281.4(BAG3):c.785C>T and c.821C>T, suggest a need for further genetic testing.
A comprehensive genetic panel to identify additional BAG3 variants may be warranted in patients with dilated cardiomyopathy 1HH and myofibrillar myopathy 6.
